sample_self_loop_rows.text: Systematic evaluation of the risks associated with combining Anafranil with other medications is lacking. Given Anafranil's primary central nervous system (CNS) effects, concurrent use with other CNS-active drugs requires prudence. Anafranil must be avoided with MAO inhibitors. When Anafranil is administered alongside anticholinergic or sympathomimetic drugs, close observation and precise dosage adjustments are mandatory. Many tricyclic antidepressants are known to antagonize the pharmacologic a

The potential risks of administering Anafranil concurrently with other medications have not undergone thorough systematic evaluation. Due to Anafranil's primary effects on the CNS, exercising caution is prudent when combining it with other CNS-active drugs. Co-administration of Anafranil with MAO inhibitors is contraindicated. When Anafranil is given with anticholinergic or sympathomimetic drugs, vigilant supervision and precise dosage adjustments are mandated. Certain tricyclic antidepressants

Aprepitant is a substrate, a moderate inhibitor, and an inducer of CYP3A4. Aprepitant is also an inducer of CYP2C9. Effect of aprepitant on the pharmacokinetics of other agents As a moderate inhibitor of CYP3A4, aprepitant can increase plasma concentrations of coadministered medicinal products that are metabolized through CYP3A4. Aprepitant has been shown to induce the metabolism of S(-) warfarin and tolbutamide, which are metabolized through CYP2C9. Coadministration of Aprepitant with these dru

Beta-adrenergic Blocking Agents: A non-comparative clinical trial involving over 1400 patients indicated that combining nifedipine and beta-blocking agents is generally well tolerated. Nevertheless, some literature reports have occasionally pointed to this combination potentially increasing the risk of congestive heart failure, severe hypotension, or aggravating angina. Long Acting Nitrates: While patients can safely co-administer nifedipine with nitrates, no controlled studies exist to assess t

The metabolism of caffeine can be disrupted by some quinolones, including ciprofloxacin, which in turn may result in reduced caffeine clearance and a longer serum half-life. For patients concurrently on cyclosporine, certain quinolones, including ciprofloxacin, have also been linked to transient increases in serum creatinine. In rare instances, severe hypoglycemia has occurred when ciprofloxacin was administered concomitantly with the sulfonylurea glyburide (Glyburide). Histamine H2-receptor ant

Due to the potential for the toxin's effect to be potentiated, the concurrent administration of BOTOX and aminoglycosides, or other agents that interfere with neuromuscular transmission (such as curare-like compounds), must be approached with caution. The impact of simultaneously or closely timed administration (within several months) of different botulinum neurotoxin serotypes remains undefined. Moreover, if another botulinum toxin is given before the effects of a prior botulinum toxin have wor

When ProSom is administered with other central nervous system-acting drugs, the pharmacology of all agents demands careful attention. The action of benzodiazepines may be enhanced by anticonvulsants, antihistamines, alcohol, barbiturates, monoamine oxidase inhibitors, narcotics, phenothiazines, psychotropic medications, or other CNS depressants. Compared to nonsmokers, smokers exhibit increased clearance of benzodiazepines, as evidenced in studies involving estazolam. While no in vivo drug-drug

Beta-adrenergic Blocking Agents: In a non-comparative clinical trial involving over 1400 patients, the concurrent use of nifedipine and beta-blocking agents was generally well tolerated. However, some literature reports have occasionally indicated that this combination might raise the risk of congestive heart failure, severe hypotension, or a worsening of angina. Long Acting Nitrates: While nifedipine can be safely given alongside nitrates, no controlled studies have assessed the antianginal eff

Aprepitant functions as a substrate, a moderate inhibitor, and an inducer of CYP3A4, in addition to being an inducer of CYP2C9. Due to its moderate inhibition of CYP3A4, aprepitant can elevate the plasma concentrations of other medications coadministered that are metabolized by CYP3A4. Furthermore, Aprepitant has been observed to induce the metabolism of S(-) warfarin and tolbutamide, both of which are metabolized via CYP2C9. Concurrent administration of Aprepitant with these drugs, or any other

The risks of using Anafranil in combination with other drugs have not been systematically evaluated. Given the primary CNS effects of Anafranil, caution is advised in using it concomitantly with other CNS-active drugs. Anafranil should not be used with MAO inhibitors. Close supervision and careful adjustment of dosage are required when Anafranil is administered with anticholinergic or sympathomimetic drugs. sample_conflict_rows.text: Antacids: When flurbiprofen was administered to young volunteers (n=12) under fasting conditions or with an antacid suspension, similar serum flurbiprofen time profiles were observed. In geriatric subjects (n=7), however, the rate of flurbiprofen absorption decreased, but its overall extent remained unchanged. Bismuth: Bismuth subsalicylate, given concomitantly with enoxacin or 60 minutes following enoxacin administration, decreased enoxacin bioavailability by approximately 25%. Thus, concomitant administration of enoxacin and bismuth subsalicylate should be avoided. Caffeine: Enoxacin is a potent inhibitor of the cytochrome P-450 isozymes responsible for the metabolism of methylxanthines. In a multiple-dose study, enoxacin caused a dose-related increase in the mean elimination half-life of caffeine,

The drug-metabolizing isozyme cytochrome P450 2D6 (debrisoquin hydroxylase) exhibits reduced biochemical activity in a segment of the Caucasian population, affecting approximately 7-10% who are termed "poor metabolizers"; reliable data on the frequency of this diminished P450 2D6 isozyme activity in Asian, African, and other populations is currently unavailable. Individuals with poor metabolizer status experience higher than anticipated plasma levels of tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) when admi

Nelfinavir is an inhibitor of CYP3A (cytochrome P450 3A). Coadministration of VIRACEPT and drugs primarily metabolized by CYP3A (e.g., dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers) may result in increased plasma concentrations of the other drug that could increase or prolong both its therapeutic and adverse effects. Nelfinavir is metabolized in proof by C.P.A. Coadministration of VIRACEPT and drugs that induce CYP3A may decrease nelfinavir plasma concentrations and reduce its therapeutic effect. Coa

Pharmacokinetic interaction between single oral doses of diltiazem and sirolimus in healthy volunteers. AIM AND BACKGROUND: The pharmacokinetic interaction between sirolimus, a macrolide immunosuppressant metabolized by CYP3A4, and the calcium channel blocker diltiazem was studied in 18 healthy subjects. Several clinically important interactions have previously been reported for other immunosuppressive drugs that are metabolized by the same enzyme and for calcium antagonists. METHODS: Healthy su

Coumarin-Type Anticoagulants: Despite several short-term controlled studies finding no significant impact of ibuprofen on prothrombin times or various other clotting factors when administered to individuals already on coumarin-type anticoagulants, physicians should be cautious. Bleeding has been reported when ibuprofen and other nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory agents were given to patients also on coumarin-type anticoagulants, hence prudence is advised when ibuprofen is administered to patients r

In a Phase I trial using escalating doses of TAXOL (110-200 mg/m2) and cisplatin (50 or 75 mg/m2) given as sequential infusions, myelosuppression was more profound when TAXOL was given after cisplatin than with the alternate sequence (ie, TAXOL before cisplatin). Pharmacokinetic data from these patients demonstrated a decrease in paclitaxel clearance of approximately 33% when TAXOL was administered following cisplatin. The metabolism of TAXOL is catalyzed by cytochrome P450 isoen-zymes CYP2C8 an

Chlorprothixene has the potential to elevate the plasma levels of co-administered lithium; therefore, close monitoring of lithium plasma concentrations is essential to prevent intoxication. When chlorprothixene is given concurrently with opioids, the opioid dose must be reduced by approximately 50%, as chlorprothixene significantly enhances both the therapeutic actions and side-effects of opioids. The simultaneous use of chlorprothixene and tramadol (Ultram) should be completely avoided due to t

Inhibitors of CYP2D6; poor metabolizers of debrisoquin: While interactions between carvedilol and potent CYP2D6 inhibitors (including quinidine, fluoxetine, paroxetine, and propafenone) remain uninvestigated, it is anticipated that these inhibitors will elevate the blood concentrations of carvedilol's R(+) enantiomer. A retrospective review of clinical trial adverse events indicated that individuals with poor 2D6 metabolism experienced more frequent dizziness during dose escalation, likely due t

Interaction of other medications with Vardenafil In vitro studies on human liver microsomes demonstrate that vardenafil undergoes metabolism primarily via cytochrome P450 (CYP) isoforms 3A4/5, with CYP2C9 playing a secondary role. Consequently, the clearance of vardenafil is expected to decrease when co-administered with inhibitors of these enzymes. Transient elevations in serum creatinine have also been associated with some quinolones, including ciprofloxacin, in patients concomitantly receiving cyclosporine. Drug interactions involving ketoprofen at daily doses of 200 mg were investigated. An elevated risk of interaction should be considered when Orudis dosages exceeding 50 mg in a single administration or 200 mg of ketoprofen daily are co-administered with highly protein-bound medications. 1. Antacids: Co-administering magnesium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide has no impact on the speed or amount of ketoprofen absorbed when given as Orudis. 2. Aspirin: While Ketoprofen does not change the absorpti

The ototoxic potential of aminoglycoside antibiotics could be increased by Furosemide, especially if renal function is impaired. This combination should be avoided unless a life-threatening situation arises. Furosemide should not be used at the same time as ethacrynic acid due to the possibility of ototoxicity. When high doses of salicylates are given concurrently with furosemide, such as in rheumatic disease, patients may experience salicylate toxicity at lower doses because of competitive rena

Concerning Drug Interactions: TAMBOCOR has been given to patients concurrently on digitalis preparations or beta-adrenergic blocking agents without experiencing adverse reactions. Moreover, TAMBOCOR has been administered to numerous patients receiving diuretics without any apparent interaction. However, when multiple oral doses of TAMBOCOR were administered to healthy subjects already stable on a maintenance dose of digoxin, a rise of 13% to 19% in plasma digoxin levels was observed six hours po

A possible drug interaction of FOSCAVIR and intravenous pentamidine has been described. Concomitant treatment of four patients in the United Kingdom with FOSCAVIR and intravenous pentamidine may have caused hypocalcemia; one patient died with severe hypocalcemia. Toxicity associated with concomitant use of aerosolized pentamidine has not been reported. Because of foscarnets tendency to cause renal impairment, the use of FOSCAVIR should be avoided in combination with potentially nephrotoxic drugs

Bismuth: Bismuth subsalicylate, when administered either concomitantly with enoxacin or 60 minutes after enoxacin administration, led to an approximate 25% decrease in enoxacin bioavailability. Therefore, concurrent administration of enoxacin and bismuth subsalicylate should be avoided. Caffeine: Enoxacin is a potent inhibitor of the cytochrome P-450 isozymes responsible for the metabolism of methylxanthines. In a multiple-dose study, enoxacin caused a dose-related increase in the mean eliminati

A few patients treated with lithium alongside HALDOL have developed an encephalopathic syndrome—manifesting as weakness, lethargy, fever, tremulousness, confusion, extrapyramidal symptoms, leukocytosis, and elevated serum enzymes, BUN, and FBS—which subsequently progressed to irreversible brain damage. Although a definitive link between these events and the concomitant use of lithium and HALDOL has not been proven, individuals undergoing this combined therapy must be meticulously observed for an

Precise renal clearance determinations for PAH are not possible with significant accuracy when patients are on sulfonamides, procaine, or thiazolesulfone. This is because these substances obstruct the vital chemical color development required for the assays. Furthermore, Probenecid inhibits the tubular secretion of specific weak acids like PAH. As a result, patients taking probenecid will exhibit falsely diminished ERPF and Tm PAH levels.

The systemic exposure to oral budesonide increased eight-fold when ketoconazole, known to inhibit CYP3A4 activity in the liver and intestinal mucosa, was given orally at the same time. A reduction in the budesonide dose should be considered if CYP3A4 activity inhibitors (e.g., ketoconazole, intraconazole, ritonavir, indinavir, saquinavir, erythromycin) are part of the treatment regimen. Systemic exposure to oral budesonide approximately doubled following substantial intake of grapefruit juice, w

Hypotension Risk: Patients on diuretics, particularly those recently started on diuretic therapy, may occasionally experience an excessive blood pressure reduction after beginning enalapril or enalaprilat. To minimize hypotensive effects with enalapril or enalaprilat, either discontinue diuretic or increase salt intake before initiating treatment. If diuretic continuation is required, provide close medical supervision for at least two hours after the initial dose and until blood pressure has sta

Cholestyramine: Concomitant cholestyramine administration decreased the mean AUC of total ezetimibe approximately 55%. The incremental LDL-C reduction due to adding ezetimibe to cholestyramine may be reduced by this interaction. Fibrates: The safety and effectiveness of ezetimibe administered with fibrates have not been established. Fibrates may increase cholesterol excretion into the bile, leading to cholelithiasis. In a preclinical study in dogs, ezetimibe increased cholesterol in the gallblad

Clinically or potentially significant drug interactions between DIFLUCAN and the following agents/classes have been observed. These are described in greater detail below: DIFLUCAN and the following agents/classes have been observed. These are described in greater detail below:  Oral hypoglycemics, Coumarin-type anticoagulants, Phenytoin, Cyclosporine, Rifampin, Theophylline, Terfenadine, Cisapride, Astemizole, Rifabutin, Tacrolimus, Short-acting benzodiazepines,  Oral hypoglycemics: Clinically s

Melatonin may interact with the following drugs: aspirin and other NSAIDs (may lower melatonin levels), fluvoxamine (bioavailability of oral melatonin is increased with coadministration), beta blockers (may decrease melatonin levels), fluoxetine (reports of psychotic episodes when coadministered), progestin (coadministration of melatonin with progestin can inhibit ovarian function in women), benzodiazepenes and other sedating drugs (may result in additive sedation and an increased incidence of a

Probenecid competes with meropenem for active tubular secretion and thus inhibits the renal excretion of meropenem. This led to statistically significant increases in the elimination half-life (38%) and in the extent of systemic exposure (56%). Therefore, the coadministration of probenecid with meropenem is not recommended. There is evidence that meropenem may reduce serum levels of valproic acid to subtherapeutic levels (therapeutic range considered to be 50 to 100    g/mL total valproate).

When methyldopa is used with other antihypertensive drugs, potentiation of antihypertensive effect may occur. Patients should be followed carefully to detect side reactions or unusual manifestations of drug idiosyncrasy. Patients may require reduced doses of anesthetics when on methyldopa. If hypotension does occur during anesthesia, it usually can be controlled by vasopressors. The adrenergic receptors remain sensitive during treatment with methyldopa. When methyldopa and lithium are given conc

The pharmacokinetic interactions listed below are potentially clinically important. Mutual inhibition of metabolism occurs with concurrent use of cyclosporin and methylprednisolone; therefore, it is possible that adverse events associated with the individual use of either drug may be more apt to occur. convulsions have been reported with concurrent use of methylprednisolone and cyclosporin. Drugs that induce hepatic enzymes such as phenobarbital, phenytoin, and rifampin may increase the clearanc

Drugs affecting pituitary or adrenocortical function, including all corticosteroid therapy, must be discontinued prior to and during testing with Metopirone. The metabolism of Metopirone is accelerated by phenytoin; therefore, results of the test may be inaccurate in patients taking phenytoin within two weeks before. Asubnormal response may occur in patients on estrogen therapy. Metopirone inhibits the glucuronidation of acetaminophen and could possibly potentiate acetaminophen toxicity.

Since MEXITIL is a substrate for the metabolic pathways involving CYP2D6 and CYP1A2 enzymes, inhibition or induction of either of these enzymes would be expected to alter mexiletine plasma concentrations. In a formal, single-dose interaction study (n = 6 males) the clearance of mexiletine was decreased by 38% following the coadministration of fluvoxamine, an inhibitor of CYP1A2. In another formal study (n = 8 extensive and n = 7 poor metabolizers of CYP2D6), coadministration of propafenone did n

A total of 11 clinical drug-drug interaction studies were conducted in healthy volunteers to evaluate the potential for interaction between MYCAMINE and mycophenolate mofetil, cyclosporine, tacrolimus, prednisolone, sirolimus, nifedipine, fluconazole, ritonavir, and rifampin. In these studies, no interaction that altered the pharmacokinetics of micafungin was observed. There was no effect of a single dose or multiple doses of MYCAMINE on mycophenolate mofetil, cyclosporine, tacrolimus, prednisol

Anticoagulants Anabolic steroids may increase sensitivity to oral anticoagulants. Dosage of the anticoagulant may have to be decreased in order to maintain desired prothrombin time. Patients receiving oral anticoagulant therapy require close monitoring, especially when anabolic steroids are started or stopped. Warfarin: A multidose study of oxandrolone, given as 5 or 10 mg BID in 15 healthy subjects concurrently treated with warfarin, resulted in a mean increase in S-warfarin half-life from 26 t
